demographics.occupation: unspecified — not stated in source (works long hours)

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: My heart keeps skipping a beat and then doing a massive, heavy thud that makes me catch my breath, and I'm terrified to fall asleep.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: It started this evening when I lay down in bed and it won't stop — I'm scared my heart is stopping or failing and I can't sleep.

symptom_narrative.onset: started this evening when I lay down in bed
symptom_narrative.duration: on and off through the evening since it started
symptom_narrative.character: it feels like my heart skips a beat, then there's a sudden strong heavy thud in my chest
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: really frightening, I feel panicked and I catch my breath with the thud, but I don't have actual pain
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: comes and goes, much more noticeable when I'm lying quietly in bed at night
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: not sure anything really helps, maybe when I'm up and moving about it's less obvious
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: lying still in bed and paying attention to my heartbeat makes it feel worse
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: I get a brief bit of lightheadedness when the heavy thud happens, but I haven't fainted, no real chest pain, and I'm not short of breath just sitting here
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: I don't think it's really happened like this before
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: I can't get to sleep because of it and I'm very worked up and anxious

social_history_volunteered.diet_and_exercise: I've had a lot of coffee today, way more than usual
social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: I've been working really long hours and I'm exhausted, and I live on my own which makes it scarier

objective_data_offered.value: feels like a normal steady beat that gets interrupted by an early strong thud and then a little pause, every now and then
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: self-palpation of the pulse by an anxious patient is subjective and cannot characterise the rhythm reliably

objective_data_offered.value: about 70 when I count it at my wrist
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: manual self-count, single reading, not device-measured

psychosocial_profile.hidden_agenda: genuinely afraid his heart is failing or about to stop; wants strong reassurance and to be told it is safe to sleep

communication_style.typical_message_style: writes anxiously, repeats fears about the heart stopping, seeks reassurance
